items.title: John Phelan out as Navy secretary, Pentagon says
items.snippet: The Pentagon provided no reason for the move.

items.title: Germany unveils strategy for becoming Europe’s strongest military by 2039
items.snippet: The Bundeswehr will move away from rigid hardware quotas − the number of tanks, aircraft or ships − toward a flexible, effects-based planning model.

items.title: Saildrone announces new USV class aimed at anti-submarine warfare
items.snippet: Spectre, a new class of unmanned surface vessel, will face sea trials in early 2027.

items.title: US Navy to integrate PAC-3 MSE interceptor missile with Aegis Combat System
items.snippet: The Navy will integrate PAC-3 MSE interceptor missiles with the Aegis Combat System, according to Lockheed Martin.

items.title: Pentagon seeks funds for Golden Dome, drones, AI in largest-ever budget request
items.snippet: According to Pentagon officials, the president's key priorities include the Golden Dome, drones, AI, data infrastructure and the defense industrial base.

items.title: US commander warns Taiwan not to ‘starve the chicken’ on defense
items.snippet: Adm. Samuel Paparo stressed the importance of Taiwan passing its stalled defense budget.

items.title: US Navy unmanned surface vessel fleet to grow sevenfold in Indo-Pacific
items.snippet: The fleet of medium unmanned surface vessels in the Indo-Pacific is expected to grow to at least 30, with smaller USVs and unmanned aerial systems.

items.title: US did not move defense system from Korea, general says
items.snippet: The Washington Post reported in March that the Pentagon was moving parts of a THAAD system to the Middle East from South Korea.

items.title: US Navy successfully tests new long-range, winged JDAM out to 200 miles
items.snippet: An April 1 test saw the munition deployed from an F/A-18 Super Hornet, fly approximately 200 miles in 34 minutes and strike within meters of its target.

items.title: Denmark to receive first SAMP/T NG air-defense system in 2028
items.snippet: Copenhagen in September picked the European system over the American-made Patriot.

items.title: After watchdog slams understaffing, AI to vet Pentagon-backed professors’ China ties
items.snippet: AI’s confusion over the nature of DOD research partnerships may mask real espionage if humans are not the final judge of foreign influence, experts warn.

items.title: US Army turns to Ukraine-tested drones to counter Iranian UAV threat
items.snippet: Army Secretary Dan Driscoll told lawmakers the service has purchased 13,000 low-cost interceptor drones to counter Iranian-made Shahed drones.

items.title: US Army wants doctrine for landing helicopters on Arctic ice
items.snippet: The Army Corps of Engineers’ research and development component is working to disprove old assumptions about the risks of landing on frozen lakes.

items.title: L3Harris announces $1 billion expansion in Virginia
items.snippet: The expansion will more than double manufacturing space at the Virginia site, which currently spans 256,000 square feet.

items.title: After 200 years scientists finally crack the “dolomite problem”
items.snippet: After two centuries of failed attempts, scientists have finally grown dolomite in the lab, cracking a long-standing geological puzzle. They discovered that the mineral’s growth stalls because of tiny ...
